# Announcing TensorStore, Chrome Root Program, and more dev news!

<SocialEmbed platform="youtube" id="4n8oeHk9dLw" />

## Description

TL;DR 313 | The Google Developer News Show

0:00 - Introduction
0:10 - New startup CPU boost improves cold starts in Cloud Run, Cloud Functions → https://goo.gle/3xUAwej  
0:34 - TensorStore for High-Performance, Scalable Array Storage → https://goo.gle/3y0lLXo  
0:58 - Announcing the Launch of the Chrome Root Program →  https://goo.gle/3xZ3Hge  
1:16 - Google Play’s $2M Indie Games Fund in Latin America → https://goo.gle/3dNYzVg  
1:43 -Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe!
 
Here to bring you the latest developer news from across Google is Rody from Developer Relations. Tune in every week for a new episode, and let us know what you think of the latest announcements in the comments below.

Follow Google Developers on Instagram → https://goo.gle/googledevs             

Watch more #DevShow → https://goo.gle/GDevShow               
Subscribe to Google Developers → https://goo.gle/developers 

#Google #Developers

## Transcript

### Introduction

**0:00** · \[MUSIC PLAYING\] SPEAKER: Hi, I'm Roadie for "The Developer Show."

**0:06** · And this is your weekly update on the coolest developer news from Google.

### New startup CPU boost improves cold starts in Cloud Run, Cloud Functions

**0:10** · First up, we have an update to Cloud Run allowing for faster cold starts with a new feature called Startup CPU boost.

**0:16** · This feature will work with Google Cloud Run and Cloud Functions, second generation.

**0:21** · With CPU boost enabled, it will allocate more CPU usage during the startup, which will help with serving requests faster.

**0:27** · For some workloads measured, startup time was cut in half.

**0:30** · And you can find out more on console.google.com.

### TensorStore for High-Performance, Scalable Array Storage

**0:34** · Next, we head over to TensorFlow to talk about the newly announced TensorStore, allowing for high performance, scalable array storage.

**0:42** · This is an open source C++ and Python library that is designed for storage manipulation of in-dimensional data.

**0:48** · This offers asynchronous API, composable indexing, and so much more.

**0:53** · You can find out more on GitHub.com/google/tensorstore.

### Announcing the Launch of the Chrome Root Program

**0:58** · Next, we can head over to Chromium to talk about the announcement of Chrome Root Program and Chrome Root Store.

**1:03** · This program determines which website certificates are trusted by default in Chrome and enables a more consistent and reliable website certification process across platforms.

**1:13** · You can learn more at blog.chromium.org.

### Google Play’s $2M Indie Games Fund in Latin America

**1:16** · And, lastly, we can talk about the newly launched Indie Games Fund in Latin America.

**1:20** · This will be awarding two million dollars in nondilutive cache awards and hands-on support for small selected game studios in Latin America.

**1:28** · This program aims to help them grow their business on Google Play and is open to indie game developers who have already published a game, even if it's already on the Play Store.

**1:36** · You can apply at developersonair.

**1:38** · withgoogle.com/e vents/indie-games-fund.

### Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe!

**1:43** · To learn more about all these week's stories, make sure to check the description box below for all the links.

**1:47** · Please remember to like, subscribe, share, and stay safe.

**1:51** · I'm Roadie for "The Developer Show."

**1:52** · Thanks for watching.

**1:53** · And we'll see you next week.

**1:55** · Hi, I'm Roadie for the-- yeah.
